target: WIN-025 detail_extra — add annotation noting dome removed this prediction
replacement_html:
Update (2026-03-15): The dome author has removed WIN-025 from confirmed predictions. A timing audit across the 2017 and 2024 eclipse events found Z-component minima uncorrelated with eclipse geometry — scatter ranged from −180 to +111 minutes relative to totality across stations. The dome attributes the anomalies to geomagnetic storm contamination (Kp ~5–6 during the 2024 eclipse), not eclipse signal. The related PROS-003 (eclipse magnetic geometry) is marked [SUSPENDED] pending a quiet eclipse day (Kp < 2).

This self-correction is scientifically honest and rare among fringe models. However, it also confirms our verdict: the dome model had no derived prediction of eclipse magnetic timing or magnitude. The observation that eclipses can produce ~10 nT variations remains explained by standard Sq current physics (Chapman 1933), which does predict that the signal tracks the shadow — but only under geomagnetically quiet conditions. The dome's inability to distinguish signal from storm noise is precisely the gap that a quantitative, geometry-derived prediction would have filled.
holes_addressed: ISS-694: Review still presents WIN-025 as current dome claim when dome has removed it
new_evidence_added: Dome wins.html 2026-03-15 removal text: Z minima scatter −180 to +111 min from totality

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome removed this voluntarily — credit them, do not use it against them
anticipated_objections.response: We explicitly credit the self-correction as scientifically honest. The annotation simply updates our review to reflect the dome current state. The analytical point — no derived prediction — was already our verdict before the removal.

anticipated_objections.objection: WIN-025 should be deleted from our review entirely since dome removed it
anticipated_objections.response: No. Our review evaluates the model as published. Removed claims are still part of the model history and illustrate its evolution. We annotate, not delete. The uncounted-failures file (FAIL-007) already tracks the removal.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author deserves genuine credit for removing predictions that failed their own quality audit. The 2024 eclipse was geomagnetically disturbed (Kp ~5-6), making magnetic variations ambiguous between eclipse and storm effects. The dome correctly identified this ambiguity rather than claiming contaminated data as confirmation. This is better scientific practice than many fringe models exhibit.
